title: Root
aliases: Root: A Game of Woodland Might and Right

component_identification.content: Root includes a double-sided map, faction boards, warrior meeples, buildings/tokens for each faction, a shared deck, dominance cards, item tokens, dice for battle, and scoring track. Core base factions are Marquise de Cat, Eyrie Dynasties, Woodland Alliance, and Vagabond, each with distinct turn structures and scoring methods.

core_game_loop.content: Players take turns following their faction board phases (typically Birdsong, Daylight, Evening). Actions include moving, battling, building, crafting, and faction-specific operations. Battles use two dice with attacker capped by number of warriors in clearing. Victory is usually first to 30 VP, though dominance cards can replace VP victory with board-state objectives.

detailed_rules.content: Rule, movement, and battle revolve around clearings and suits. You rule a clearing when you have the most combined warriors/buildings there, with ties favoring current ruler logic per rules. Crafting requires matching crafting pieces in suited clearings and grants persistent effects, items, or immediate points. Marquise runs an action-economy engine through sawmills/workshops/recruiters and scores by building placement. Eyrie programs actions into a decree; failure to perform required actions causes turmoil, losing points but changing leader powers. Woodland Alliance spreads sympathy, incites revolts, and leverages supporter cards, often scoring in bursts as sympathy expands. Vagabond moves through forests/clearings, manages item exhaustion/damage, completes quests, aids factions, and can become hostile for battle-focused scoring. Card draw, ambushes, and table talk are pivotal because asymmetry creates kingmaking pressure and shifting threat levels.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Standard win condition is reaching 30 VP at end of a turn segment where points are scored. Dominance cards, once activated, set alternate win conditions such as ruling specific corners or coalitions with another player. If multiple triggers conflict, timing windows in faction rules determine resolution; generally active player checks victory during their turn.

beginner_strategy.content: Learn one faction deeply before rotating; Root rewards faction mastery more than generic tactics. Track who can score fastest in the next round, not just current VP leader. Keep mobility and battle reach to police runaway engines early. Craft only when it advances your faction tempo; inefficient crafting can cost critical actions. For new tables, communicate threat assessments openly to reduce accidental kingmaking.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 343; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000); Check 6 failed: source_verified is not true
